title: Terminal-output overlap probe
hypothesis: The fused softmax terminal-output path may still be beneficial, but its gain is currently hidden either by overlap in the perf/execution path or by a benchmark that does not make terminal output traffic part of the critical path. A bounded follow-on probe should determine which explanation is dominating before any broader architecture sweep is attempted.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does fused terminal output show a measurable benefit once the comparison is restricted to a terminal-output-sensitive proof setup rather than the original tiny softmax-tail benchmark alone?
include: non-fused vs fused softmax-tail execution on one fixed architecture under a terminal-output-sensitive setup
trace-based overlap inspection tied to the same direct comparison
exclude: broad multi-workload sweep before the mechanism-level question is answered
follow_on_broad_sweep: reintroduce broader workload and architecture comparisons only if the focused overlap-sensitive proof is positive or remains ambiguous

expected_benefit: clarifies whether fused-output benefit is real but hidden
prevents broad reruns that do not answer the mechanism question
improves future first-stage evaluation design for terminal-op proposals
risks: the benchmark redesign could accidentally broaden into a workload-suite effort
perf-trace inference could still be ambiguous if the overlap model is not directly exposed
the follow-on may show that the fused mechanism is not practically important on current workloads
